Description: ## Summary Closes #344.  The `scroll` action dispatched a page-level wheel event at the current mouse position, so scrolling a focused listbox or an overflow-scroll div moved the outer page instead of the element. `scroll_at` was a coordinate-based workaround; this PR adds the bid-based variant so agents can target an element directly, mirroring the other bid-taking actions (`click`, `fill`, `focus`, ...).  ```python scroll(0, 200)                 # unchanged: scrolls the page scroll(0, 200, bid='a17')      # new: scrolls inside that element ```  ## Design notes - **Backward compatible.** `bid` is an optional keyword-only-in-practice parameter (keeps existing `scroll(dx, dy)` calls in webarena / miniwob / workarena subsets working). Happy to switch to `scroll(bid, dx, dy)` if maintainers prefer, but that is a breaking change across every action subset. - **Uses `Element.scrollBy`, not a synthetic `WheelEvent`.** Untrusted wheel events do not trigger native scrolling, and a bubbling synthetic event would let page-level handlers cancel a scroll that was meant for the sub-element. The trade-off - sites that implement custom scrolling inside `wheel` listeners (virtualized lists, some carousels) are not exercised by this path - is called out in the docstring. - **`scroll_into_view=False`** in the bid path: the whole point is to not move the outer page, even in demo mode.  ## Test plan All three new tests pass locally against Chromium:  - [x] `tests/core/test_actions_highlevel.py::test_scroll_in_sub_element` - listbox and scrollable div scroll via `scroll(bid=...)` while `window.scrollY` stays `0`; the plain `scroll(dx, dy)` still scrolls the page. - [x] `tests/core/test_actions_highlevel.py::test_scroll_without_bid_ignores_focused_listbox` - regression test pinning down the symptom that motivated the API change: focusing a listbox does not make `scroll(dx, dy)` (no bid) scroll that listbox. - [x] `tests/miniwob/test_click-scroll-list.py::test_scroll_with_bid_scrolls_listbox` - integration test against `miniwob.click-scroll-list` (the task #344 references). Proves the bid path actually moves `<select size=N>.scrollTop`, while the no-bid path does not.  Pre-existing failures on `main` (`test_cheat[0-4]`, `test_goto`, `test_mouse_down_up`, `test_scroll`) are unrelated and reproduce without this PR.  ## Files - `browsergym/core/src/browsergym/core/action/functions.py` - `scroll(delta_x, delta_y, bid=None)` - `docs/src/core/action_space.rst` - doc entry updated - `tests/core/data/scroll_list.html` - new fixture - `tests/core/test_actions_highlevel.py` - two new tests - `tests/miniwob/test_click-scroll-list.py` - miniwob integration test
- Better python env caching using uv. - Remove sigstore (not needed as we are using secure publishing in pypi)

Why call the evaluator at every step? In the official implementation, it only evaluates at the final step.  https://github.com/ServiceNow/BrowserGym/blob/ec6b802cd655f2c6a84ebd66a22a4435d8147272/browsergym/webarena/src/browsergym/webarena/task.py#L185C9-L185C11  https://github.com/web-arena-x/webarena/blob/df352854eef255b007110948f6d4f539af039717/run.py#L330
Discuss Information: Browsergym was originally designed without a stop action, which is why we call the evaluator at every step to detect if the task is done or not, from the environment side. It could be nice to have this (optional) stop action so that agents can run faster on webarena. | > Browsergym was originally designed without a stop action, which is why we call the evaluator at every step to detect if the task is done or not, from the environment side. It could be nice to have this (optional) stop action so that agents can run faster on webarena.   I fully understand the idea of separating the environment implementation from specific task logic. However, there are two potential issues with this approach:    1. Evaluating at every step might lead to the model stopping prematurely, such as when it simply lands on the answer page, which could inadvertently leak labels.     2. Some evaluations in WebArena involve navigating away from the current page, which could result in losing unfinished operations on that page. The current implementation of page preservation in WebArena is ineffective (and I suspect it might be ineffective for most environments, as pages cannot be easily copied).    A possible solution would be to modify the evaluation implementation in the WebArena environment, ensuring that the actual evaluator is only invoked after the termination conditions are met. | We agree with this, in recent future we will provide variants where the agent is responsible for stopping.

Description: # Pull Request Description - #298  ## Summary of Changes: - Added the `download_file` function to handle file downloads by clicking a specified element (`bid`). - Ensures the download directory exists, monitors progress, and saves the file upon completion.    ## Reason for Changes: - Automates the download process with real-time progress updates and saves the file to a specified directory.  ### Example: - Starting Download: ```Starting download...```  - During Download: ``` Downloading... Elapsed time: 1 seconds Downloading... Elapsed time: 2 seconds ```  - On Completion: ```Download completed: downloads/my_report.pdf```  ## How to Test: 1. Call `download_file` with a valid `bid` and optional `download_path`. 2. Verify the download starts and the file is saved correctly.
